articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and export capabilities.
• This phase emphasizes R&D in electrolyser manufacturing, developing green hydrogen hubs, and creating a robust export infrastructure.
• A new 'Green Hydrogen Innovation Fund' of ₹5,000 crore has been announced to support startups and research institutions.
• The mission aims to reduce carbon emissions by 50 MT annually and create over 6 lakh jobs by 2030, positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• Incentives for domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen will be streamlined under a new 'Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition' (SIGHT) scheme.

Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Expedite Comprehensive Data Privacy Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated the urgent need for a robust data privacy framework, emphasizing its link to the fundamental right to privacy.
• The Court directed the government to swiftly implement the pending provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and address implementation gaps.
• Concerns were raised regarding data localization requirements, cross-border data flows, and the accountability of data fiduciaries.
• The ruling came in response to a petition challenging certain aspects of data collection by private entities and government agencies.
• This judgment is expected to accelerate the formulation of rules and regulations under the DPDP Act, providing clarity to businesses and citizens.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the seventh consecutive time.
• The decision reflects continued caution regarding inflation, despite a recent moderation, and aims to anchor inflation expectations.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price volatility as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to gradually withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate a possible rate cut later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflation and stable economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate.
Inflation target set at 4% with a +/- 2% band.

mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on interest rates and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Examine the role of global economic factors and domestic supply-side issues in shaping India's inflation trajectory.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing economic cycles in India.
Impact of monetary policy on various sectors like housing, industry, and exports.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo prominently displayed with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Show Significant Progress, Key Chapters Finalized
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered a crucial phase, with several key chapters reportedly finalized.
• Both sides expressed optimism about concluding the comprehensive agreement by year-end, which could significantly boost bilateral trade and investment.
• Chapters on goods, services, and investment are seeing substantial breakthroughs, though intellectual property rights and sustainability clauses remain areas of intense discussion.
• The FTA aims to reduce tariffs, open market access, and establish robust mechanisms for trade facilitation and dispute resolution.
• A successful FTA would enhance India's strategic partnership with the EU, diversifying its trade relationships amidst global economic shifts.

articles.headline: ISRO Unveils Roadmap for Chandrayaan-4, Eyes Lunar Sample Return Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced its ambitious roadmap for Chandrayaan-4, aiming for a lunar sample return mission by the end of the decade.
• This mission will involve a more advanced lander and rover, along with a dedicated orbiter designed for precise sample collection and return to Earth.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the lessons learned from Chandrayaan-3's successful soft landing and Chandrayaan-2's orbital mission.
• International collaborations are being explored for specific technologies and deep-space communication support.
• The mission underscores India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its commitment to advancing lunar science.

Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

Chandrayaan-2: Orbiter successful, lander/rover failed (2019).

ISRO's future plans include Gaganyaan (human spaceflight) and Shukrayaan (Venus mission).

articles.headline: Government Accelerates Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Expansion for Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced a renewed push for expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The focus is on leveraging Aadhaar, UPI, and the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) to reach unbanked populations and small businesses.
• New initiatives include simplified KYC processes for rural areas and localized digital literacy programs.
• The expansion aims to further financial inclusion, streamline government services, and foster a more equitable digital economy.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to develop innovative applications built on the DPI stack, ensuring broader adoption.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Electoral Reforms Ahead of 2029 General Elections
articles.explainer:
• A bipartisan Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending significant electoral reforms, including state funding of elections and a cap on individual donations.
• The report also proposed strengthening the Election Commission's powers, particularly concerning de-registration of political parties and enforcing the Model Code of Conduct.
• Emphasis was placed on increasing voter turnout, especially among urban and youth populations, through digital initiatives and awareness campaigns.
• Recommendations include a transparent mechanism for electoral bonds, or their replacement with an alternative funding model.
• These reforms aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and fairness in India's electoral process, addressing long-standing concerns.

articles.headline: India's Tiger Conservation Efforts Yield Positive Results, New Report Highlights Challenges
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) indicates a continued increase in tiger population across several reserves.
• The report attributes the success to stringent anti-poaching measures, habitat restoration, and community involvement in conservation efforts.
• However, it also highlighted challenges such as human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation due to infrastructure projects, and the impact of climate change.
• Recommendations include enhancing corridor connectivity, adopting advanced surveillance technologies, and strengthening transboundary cooperation for tiger protection.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, making its conservation efforts globally significant.

articles.headline: PM GatiShakti Platform Integrated for Urban Planning and Smart City Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced the integration of the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan platform into urban planning processes.
• This move aims to bring synergy and efficiency in developing urban infrastructure projects, including smart cities, metro networks, and urban transport.
• The platform will enable data-driven decision-making, reducing delays and optimizing resource allocation for complex urban projects.
• Training programs for urban planners and administrators on utilizing the GatiShakti framework are being rolled out nationwide.
• The integration seeks to ensure multi-modal connectivity and holistic development, aligning urban growth with national infrastructure goals.

articles.headline: India's G20 Chairmanship Focuses on Climate Finance Mobilization for Developing Nations
articles.explainer:
• As India assumes the G20 chairmanship, its primary agenda includes mobilizing substantial climate finance for developing and least developed countries.
• The focus is on reforming multilateral development banks (MDBs) to enhance their capacity for climate financing and risk mitigation.
• India is advocating for a clear roadmap for developed nations to meet their $100 billion annual climate finance commitment.
• Discussions are underway on innovative financing mechanisms, including green bonds and blended finance, to bridge the funding gap.
• This leadership position allows India to champion the concerns of the Global South on climate justice and equitable energy transition.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog published a comprehensive discussion paper outlining a proposed national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework focuses on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and data privacy in the development and deployment of AI systems.
• It addresses concerns about algorithmic bias, job displacement, and the potential misuse of AI technologies.
• The paper invites feedback from stakeholders, including industry, academia, and civil society, to formulate robust policies.
• India aims to position itself as a responsible leader in AI innovation, balancing technological advancement with ethical considerations.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Funding for Coastal Erosion Mitigation and Mangrove Restoration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced a significant increase in funding for coastal erosion mitigation projects.
• The enhanced allocation will support sea wall construction, sand dune restoration, and large-scale mangrove plantation drives in vulnerable coastal states.
• The initiative recognizes the dual benefit of mangroves in protecting coastlines and serving as carbon sinks, contributing to climate goals.
• Collaborations with local communities and scientific institutions are being strengthened for effective implementation and monitoring.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard coastal communities and ecosystems from the escalating impacts of climate change and sea-level rise.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in North-Eastern States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated development of critical infrastructure along India's northern and eastern borders, particularly in the North-Eastern states.
• Projects include upgrading roads, tunnels, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance troop mobility and logistical support.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to bolster national security and improve connectivity in strategically important and remote regions.
• Special emphasis is being given to integrating local communities into the development process, ensuring their livelihoods are protected.
• This push aims to counter aggressive posturing by neighboring countries and facilitate economic development in border areas.

articles.headline: India Seeks Review of Indus Water Treaty Provisions with Pakistan Amid Hydropower Disputes
articles.explainer:
• India has formally requested a review of certain provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over Pakistan's objections to Indian hydropower projects.
• The request focuses on the interpretation of technical aspects related to run-of-the-river projects on the Western rivers allocated to Pakistan.
• India asserts its right to utilize its share of water for non-consumptive uses like hydropower generation, as per the treaty.
• The move comes after prolonged disagreements and a lack of resolution through the Permanent Indus Commission.
• While emphasizing adherence to the treaty, India aims to ensure its developmental needs are met, particularly for energy and irrigation.

articles.headline: New Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development in Phase II
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced the commencement of Phase II implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a strong emphasis on vocational training and skill development.
• Initiatives include integrating vocational courses from middle school, establishing skill hubs in higher education institutions, and industry partnerships for apprenticeships.
• The goal is to equip students with market-relevant skills, reduce unemployment, and enhance employability in emerging sectors.
• A national credit framework for vocational education is being developed to ensure seamless transition between academic and vocational streams.
• This phase aims to address the skill gap in the workforce and leverage India's demographic dividend effectively.

articles.headline: ICAR Launches New Climate-Resilient Agriculture Technologies for Small Farmers
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) unveiled a suite of new climate-resilient agricultural technologies aimed at enhancing the adaptive capacity of small and marginal farmers.
• These innovations include drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation systems, and climate-smart farming practices.
• The technologies are designed to minimize yield losses due to extreme weather events and improve resource efficiency.
• Extensive farmer training programs and demonstration plots are being established through Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) for wider adoption.
• This initiative is crucial for ensuring food security and farmer livelihoods in the face of escalating climate change impacts.

articles.headline: Government Expands Incentives for Semiconductor Fab and Display Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced an expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for semiconductor and display manufacturing.
• The revised policy offers higher fiscal support and streamlined approval processes to attract more global players and domestic investments.
• The goal is to establish India as a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
• Focus areas include advanced packaging, compound semiconductors, and mature nodes for critical applications.

articles.headline: Major Cities Develop Action Plans to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities, including Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai, have initiated comprehensive action plans to mitigate the urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• Strategies include increasing green cover, promoting cool roofs and pavements, improving urban design for better ventilation, and managing waste heat.
• The plans are a response to rising temperatures and increased heatwave frequency, which pose significant public health and economic risks.
• Public awareness campaigns are being launched to educate residents on heat stress management and energy-efficient building practices.
• These local-level initiatives are crucial for urban climate resilience and aligning with national climate adaptation goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Climate Change.
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island effect refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.

National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC).

articles.headline: Government Boosts Health-Tech Startups with New Incubation and Funding Schemes
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launched new schemes to support health-tech startups.
• The initiatives include dedicated incubation centers, seed funding, and access to government healthcare infrastructure for pilot projects.
• The focus is on innovations in telemedicine, AI-powered diagnostics, wearable health devices, and affordable medical technology.
• The schemes aim to leverage technology for improving healthcare access, affordability, and quality across India.
• This push is crucial for achieving Universal Health Coverage and strengthening India's position as a global healthcare innovation hub.

Analyze the government's role in fostering an ecosystem for health-tech innovation through funding, incubation, and policy support.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India to Conserve Ancient Silk Route Heritage Sites
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced a new project for the conservation and restoration of ancient heritage sites along the Indian stretch of the historic Silk Route.
• The initiative focuses on sites in Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, and parts of North-East India, which served as crucial trade and cultural exchange points.
• The project involves extensive archaeological surveys, structural conservation, and documentation of historical artifacts.
• It aims to preserve India's rich cultural legacy, promote heritage tourism, and foster greater understanding of ancient trade networks.
• International collaborations are being explored to share expertise and integrate these sites into a broader 'Silk Road Cultural Heritage Corridor'.

Major trade goods included silk, spices, precious stones.

India was a significant part of the Southern Silk Route.

Examine the potential for international collaboration in preserving the shared heritage of the Silk Route.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM), consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to produce 5 million metric tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
2. The 'Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition' (SIGHT) scheme provides incentives for both domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
3. The NGHM primarily focuses on reducing carbon emissions from the transportation sector only.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The NGHM aims for 5 MMT annual production by 2030. Statement 2 is correct. The SIGHT scheme is a key component providing financial incentives. Statement 3 is incorrect. The NGHM aims to reduce carbon emissions across various sectors, not just transportation, by promoting green hydrogen as a clean energy source in industries, power generation, and transportation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) stack?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and ONDC are foundational components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) or India Stack. GSTN (Goods and Services Tax Network) is a non-profit, non-government company that manages the IT system for the GST, but it is not typically categorized as a core DPI like Aadhaar or UPI, which provide foundational identity and payment layers for a broad range of digital services.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Water Treaty (IWT):
1. The Treaty was brokered by the United Nations in 1960.
2. India has exclusive use of the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej).
3. Pakistan has exclusive use of the waters of the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab), but India can use them for non-consumptive purposes.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Indus Water Treaty (IWT) was brokered by the World Bank (then the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development), not the United Nations. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, defining the allocation of Eastern and Western Rivers between India and Pakistan as per the treaty.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory and institutionalized framework under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, for maintaining price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth. It is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to contain inflation within the specified target level.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in cities is primarily caused by which of the following?
1. Abundance of green spaces and water bodies.
2. Dark-colored surfaces like asphalt and concrete absorbing solar radiation.
3. Heat generated from human activities and air conditioning systems.
4. Increased air circulation due to tall buildings.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 2 and 3 are correct. Dark-colored surfaces (asphalt, concrete) absorb more solar radiation, and anthropogenic heat from vehicles, industries, and air conditioners contribute significantly to the UHI effect. Statement 1 is incorrect as green spaces and water bodies actually help reduce UHI. Statement 4 is incorrect; tall buildings can often reduce air circulation, trapping heat, rather than increasing it.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) to transform the country into a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. What are the key challenges and opportunities in achieving this vision?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation, environmental impact assessment; Investment models.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NGHM and its objectives. Discuss opportunities like renewable energy potential, strategic location, and policy support. Analyze challenges such as high production costs, technology scaling, infrastructure development, and competition. Conclude with policy recommendations for successful implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's directive on expediting a comprehensive data privacy framework underscores the judiciary's role in safeguarding fundamental rights in the digital age. Discuss the constitutional basis for the right to privacy in India and analyze the challenges in enacting and implementing a robust data protection law.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by referencing the Puttaswamy judgment and Article 21. Explain the evolution of the right to privacy. Detail the challenges in implementing data protection laws, including balancing state surveillance, corporate interests, technological complexities, and public awareness. Discuss the role of the DPDP Act, 2023, and suggest ways to strengthen its implementation.

mainsMocks.question: India's accelerated border infrastructure development in its North-Eastern states serves both national security and regional development objectives. Evaluate this dual purpose, discussing the strategic importance of the region and the challenges encountered in infrastructure projects along the border.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Internal Security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the context of border infrastructure in the North-East. Explain its strategic importance for national security, countering external threats, and internal stability. Elaborate on how it contributes to regional development, connectivity (Act East Policy), and economic integration. Discuss challenges like difficult terrain, environmental concerns, land acquisition, and geopolitical sensitivities. Conclude with a balanced perspective on balancing security and development.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Expedite Comprehensive Data Privacy Framework
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated the urgent need for a robust data privacy framework, emphasizing its link to the fundamental right to privacy.
• The Court directed the government to swiftly implement the pending provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and address implementation gaps.
• Concerns were raised regarding data localization requirements, cross-border data flows, and the accountability of data fiduciaries.
• The ruling came in response to a petition challenging certain aspects of data collection by private entities and government agencies.
• This judgment is expected to accelerate the formulation of rules and regulations under the DPDP Act, providing clarity to businesses and citizens.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Talks Show Significant Progress, Key Chapters Finalized
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered a crucial phase, with several key chapters reportedly finalized.
• Both sides expressed optimism about concluding the comprehensive agreement by year-end, which could significantly boost bilateral trade and investment.
• Chapters on goods, services, and investment are seeing substantial breakthroughs, though intellectual property rights and sustainability clauses remain areas of intense discussion.
• The FTA aims to reduce tariffs, open market access, and establish robust mechanisms for trade facilitation and dispute resolution.
• A successful FTA would enhance India's strategic partnership with the EU, diversifying its trade relationships amidst global economic shifts.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Electoral Reforms Ahead of 2029 General Elections
GS2.explainer:
• A bipartisan Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending significant electoral reforms, including state funding of elections and a cap on individual donations.
• The report also proposed strengthening the Election Commission's powers, particularly concerning de-registration of political parties and enforcing the Model Code of Conduct.
• Emphasis was placed on increasing voter turnout, especially among urban and youth populations, through digital initiatives and awareness campaigns.
• Recommendations include a transparent mechanism for electoral bonds, or their replacement with an alternative funding model.
• These reforms aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and fairness in India's electoral process, addressing long-standing concerns.

GS2.headline: India's G20 Chairmanship Focuses on Climate Finance Mobilization for Developing Nations
GS2.explainer:
• As India assumes the G20 chairmanship, its primary agenda includes mobilizing substantial climate finance for developing and least developed countries.
• The focus is on reforming multilateral development banks (MDBs) to enhance their capacity for climate financing and risk mitigation.
• India is advocating for a clear roadmap for developed nations to meet their $100 billion annual climate finance commitment.
• Discussions are underway on innovative financing mechanisms, including green bonds and blended finance, to bridge the funding gap.
• This leadership position allows India to champion the concerns of the Global South on climate justice and equitable energy transition.

GS2.headline: India Seeks Review of Indus Water Treaty Provisions with Pakistan Amid Hydropower Disputes
GS2.explainer:
• India has formally requested a review of certain provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over Pakistan's objections to Indian hydropower projects.
• The request focuses on the interpretation of technical aspects related to run-of-the-river projects on the Western rivers allocated to Pakistan.
• India asserts its right to utilize its share of water for non-consumptive uses like hydropower generation, as per the treaty.
• The move comes after prolonged disagreements and a lack of resolution through the Permanent Indus Commission.
• While emphasizing adherence to the treaty, India aims to ensure its developmental needs are met, particularly for energy and irrigation.

GS2.headline: New Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development in Phase II
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced the commencement of Phase II implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a strong emphasis on vocational training and skill development.
• Initiatives include integrating vocational courses from middle school, establishing skill hubs in higher education institutions, and industry partnerships for apprenticeships.
• The goal is to equip students with market-relevant skills, reduce unemployment, and enhance employability in emerging sectors.
• A national credit framework for vocational education is being developed to ensure seamless transition between academic and vocational streams.
• This phase aims to address the skill gap in the workforce and leverage India's demographic dividend effectively.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and export capabilities.
• This phase emphasizes R&D in electrolyser manufacturing, developing green hydrogen hubs, and creating a robust export infrastructure.
• A new 'Green Hydrogen Innovation Fund' of ₹5,000 crore has been announced to support startups and research institutions.
• The mission aims to reduce carbon emissions by 50 MT annually and create over 6 lakh jobs by 2030, positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
• Incentives for domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen will be streamlined under a new 'Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition' (SIGHT) scheme.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the seventh consecutive time.
• The decision reflects continued caution regarding inflation, despite a recent moderation, and aims to anchor inflation expectations.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price volatility as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to gradually withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate a possible rate cut later in the year, contingent on sustained disinflation and stable economic indicators.

GS3.headline: ISRO Unveils Roadmap for Chandrayaan-4, Eyes Lunar Sample Return Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced its ambitious roadmap for Chandrayaan-4, aiming for a lunar sample return mission by the end of the decade.
• This mission will involve a more advanced lander and rover, along with a dedicated orbiter designed for precise sample collection and return to Earth.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the lessons learned from Chandrayaan-3's successful soft landing and Chandrayaan-2's orbital mission.
• International collaborations are being explored for specific technologies and deep-space communication support.
• The mission underscores India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its commitment to advancing lunar science.

GS3.headline: Government Accelerates Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Expansion for Financial Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced a renewed push for expanding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The focus is on leveraging Aadhaar, UPI, and the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) to reach unbanked populations and small businesses.
• New initiatives include simplified KYC processes for rural areas and localized digital literacy programs.
• The expansion aims to further financial inclusion, streamline government services, and foster a more equitable digital economy.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to develop innovative applications built on the DPI stack, ensuring broader adoption.

GS3.headline: India's Tiger Conservation Efforts Yield Positive Results, New Report Highlights Challenges
GS3.explainer:
• A new report released by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) indicates a continued increase in tiger population across several reserves.
• The report attributes the success to stringent anti-poaching measures, habitat restoration, and community involvement in conservation efforts.
• However, it also highlighted challenges such as human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation due to infrastructure projects, and the impact of climate change.
• Recommendations include enhancing corridor connectivity, adopting advanced surveillance technologies, and strengthening transboundary cooperation for tiger protection.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, making its conservation efforts globally significant.

GS3.trivia: Tigers are solitary animals and typically hunt at night.

GS3.headline: PM GatiShakti Platform Integrated for Urban Planning and Smart City Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced the integration of the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan platform into urban planning processes.
• This move aims to bring synergy and efficiency in developing urban infrastructure projects, including smart cities, metro networks, and urban transport.
• The platform will enable data-driven decision-making, reducing delays and optimizing resource allocation for complex urban projects.
• Training programs for urban planners and administrators on utilizing the GatiShakti framework are being rolled out nationwide.
• The integration seeks to ensure multi-modal connectivity and holistic development, aligning urban growth with national infrastructure goals.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog published a comprehensive discussion paper outlining a proposed national framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance.
• The framework focuses on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and data privacy in the development and deployment of AI systems.
• It addresses concerns about algorithmic bias, job displacement, and the potential misuse of AI technologies.
• The paper invites feedback from stakeholders, including industry, academia, and civil society, to formulate robust policies.
• India aims to position itself as a responsible leader in AI innovation, balancing technological advancement with ethical considerations.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Funding for Coastal Erosion Mitigation and Mangrove Restoration
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced a significant increase in funding for coastal erosion mitigation projects.
• The enhanced allocation will support sea wall construction, sand dune restoration, and large-scale mangrove plantation drives in vulnerable coastal states.
• The initiative recognizes the dual benefit of mangroves in protecting coastlines and serving as carbon sinks, contributing to climate goals.
• Collaborations with local communities and scientific institutions are being strengthened for effective implementation and monitoring.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard coastal communities and ecosystems from the escalating impacts of climate change and sea-level rise.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in North-Eastern States
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced accelerated development of critical infrastructure along India's northern and eastern borders, particularly in the North-Eastern states.
• Projects include upgrading roads, tunnels, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance troop mobility and logistical support.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to bolster national security and improve connectivity in strategically important and remote regions.
• Special emphasis is being given to integrating local communities into the development process, ensuring their livelihoods are protected.
• This push aims to counter aggressive posturing by neighboring countries and facilitate economic development in border areas.

GS3.headline: ICAR Launches New Climate-Resilient Agriculture Technologies for Small Farmers
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) unveiled a suite of new climate-resilient agricultural technologies aimed at enhancing the adaptive capacity of small and marginal farmers.
• These innovations include drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation systems, and climate-smart farming practices.
• The technologies are designed to minimize yield losses due to extreme weather events and improve resource efficiency.
• Extensive farmer training programs and demonstration plots are being established through Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) for wider adoption.
• This initiative is crucial for ensuring food security and farmer livelihoods in the face of escalating climate change impacts.

GS3.headline: Government Expands Incentives for Semiconductor Fab and Display Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced an expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for semiconductor and display manufacturing.
• The revised policy offers higher fiscal support and streamlined approval processes to attract more global players and domestic investments.
• The goal is to establish India as a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
• Focus areas include advanced packaging, compound semiconductors, and mature nodes for critical applications.
• This move is critical for India's digital transformation, defense, and strategic sectors, ensuring a resilient supply chain.

GS3.headline: Major Cities Develop Action Plans to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
GS3.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities, including Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai, have initiated comprehensive action plans to mitigate the urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• Strategies include increasing green cover, promoting cool roofs and pavements, improving urban design for better ventilation, and managing waste heat.
• The plans are a response to rising temperatures and increased heatwave frequency, which pose significant public health and economic risks.
• Public awareness campaigns are being launched to educate residents on heat stress management and energy-efficient building practices.
• These local-level initiatives are crucial for urban climate resilience and aligning with national climate adaptation goals.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Health-Tech Startups with New Incubation and Funding Schemes
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launched new schemes to support health-tech startups.
• The initiatives include dedicated incubation centers, seed funding, and access to government healthcare infrastructure for pilot projects.
• The focus is on innovations in telemedicine, AI-powered diagnostics, wearable health devices, and affordable medical technology.
• The schemes aim to leverage technology for improving healthcare access, affordability, and quality across India.
• This push is crucial for achieving Universal Health Coverage and strengthening India's position as a global healthcare innovation hub.
